llm_engineering_lab.title: LLM Engineering Lab
llm_engineering_lab.summary: Portfolio of 11 production-grade LLM systems built from scratch — RAG at 800k-document scale, QLoRA fine-tuning, multi-agent orchestration, autonomous planning, serverless cloud deployment. Use this key for lab-overview questions; use the per-project keys for implementation depth on any single sub-project.

llm_price_predictor.title: LLM Price Predictor
llm_price_predictor.summary: Flagship of the LLM Engineering Lab. Real-time autonomous deal-finding system: 820k Amazon products, full ML lifecycle, RAG over 800k documents, QLoRA fine-tuning of Llama-3.2-3B, ensemble (GPT-5.1+RAG / fine-tuned Modal specialist / DNN), LLM-orchestrated autonomous agent, live Gradio dashboard. Final ensemble: MAE $29.95, R² 86.3%.

expert_knowledge_worker.title: Expert Knowledge Worker (RAG Chatbot)
expert_knowledge_worker.summary: RAG assistant for a company knowledge base. Two interchangeable pipelines: LangChain baseline and from-scratch optimised pipeline with LLM-based chunking (headline+summary+verbatim), hierarchical RAG via category summaries, query rewriting, dual retrieval pass, LLM reranking. Standalone evaluation dashboard (MRR, nDCG, LLM-as-judge).

web_summary_tool.title: Web Summary Tool
web_summary_tool.summary: Embeddable Python utility that turns a webpage URL into a concise Markdown summary. Persona/tone control via parameter; dual backend (OpenAI hosted + Ollama local); designed for internal workflows that need quick, repeatable briefs from unstructured web content.

company_brochure_generator.title: Company Brochure Generator
company_brochure_generator.summary: Two-stage agentic workflow that turns a company website into a Markdown brochure. Stage 1: LLM selects brochure-relevant pages (planning/routing). Stage 2: LLM writes the brochure using retrieved page text. Optional translation. Demonstrates the select-then-generate pattern.

tech_tutor.title: Tech Tutor
tech_tutor.summary: Python utility that explains data-work concepts and code through a single configurable movie-analogy thread (with technical translations alongside). Designed for fast learning loops; dual backend (OpenAI + Ollama). Analogy-as-backbone pattern, not analogy-as-decoration.

multi_agent_conversation.title: Multi-Agent Conversation
multi_agent_conversation.summary: Three-agent review-panel orchestration (Staff Data Scientist, Product Manager, Tech Lead). Learning lab for the engineering pitfalls of multi-agent systems: stale context, role drift, duplicated state updates, turn-taking discipline. Synthesis-as-explicit-role pattern via the Tech Lead.

sales_intake_copilot.title: Sales Intake Copilot
sales_intake_copilot.summary: B2B sales-intake chatbot that qualifies a lead in a few turns and produces a structured handoff note for a human sales rep. Bifurcated output (conversational + structured) from a single LLM under prompt contract; faithfulness discipline forbids inventing missing details.

flight_booking_agentic_tool.title: Flight Booking Agentic Tool
flight_booking_agentic_tool.summary: Tool-calling agent backed by stateful SQLite. Quote ticket prices, ask for confirmation, create mock bookings with autoincrement IDs. Demonstrates structured tool schemas, tool-call loop discipline, and multimodal output (text + TTS audio + destination image).

meeting_minute_generator.title: Meeting Minute Generator
meeting_minute_generator.summary: Two-stage pipeline: Whisper audio transcription, then LLM Markdown minutes generation under a fixed-structure prompt contract. Faithfulness guardrails (missing info marked 'Not specified', not invented), low temperature, transcript persistence for traceability.

synthetic_ab_dataset_generator.title: Synthetic A/B Dataset Generator
synthetic_ab_dataset_generator.summary: Gradio app that generates a synthetic A/B conversion CSV plus a Markdown dataset card from configurable knobs. Schema-as-contract enforcement; both artefacts persisted to disk. Designed for prototyping dashboards, testing analytics pipelines, and teaching experimentation concepts.

llm_code_performance_benchmark.title: LLM Code Performance Benchmark
llm_code_performance_benchmark.summary: Multi-model benchmark on the Python→C++ speedup task. Compares OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama / OpenRouter on the same workload; failure modes are first-class outputs (compile error vs runtime error vs success); each model's generated C++ persisted as an artefact for inspection.

ai_jie.title: AI-JIE — Automated Job Information Extraction
ai_jie.summary: Structured extraction pipeline turning raw job-posting text into validated, intent-classified Job objects. Chain-of-thought scaffolding (intermediate Pydantic fields force extract-then-classify) was the largest accuracy gain across 33 prompt versions. Two-tier eval (LLM-as-judge + human, 4.11/5 overall). 3,892 DS postings published to HuggingFace Hub.

job_intelligence_engine.title: Job Intelligence Engine
job_intelligence_engine.summary: Deterministic end-to-end job-market intelligence system. Converts postings into market signals (skill demand, salary drivers) and a constraint-aware recommender (best-now vs stretch roles) with counterfactual upskilling ranked by simulated lift. Streamlit app live; pipeline-chapters-with-stable-outputs architecture.

mlb_analytics_sql.title: MLB Analytics — Lahman Database (1871–2024)
mlb_analytics_sql.summary: End-to-end SQL analytics on 150 years of MLB history. Manual relational schema, reusable analytical views, three query tiers (analysis / advanced / optimised), window functions, multi-step CTE pipelines. Four analytical pillars: talent pipelines, salary dynamics, career trajectories, physical profiles.

python_ml_projects.title: Python ML Projects — Core ML Algorithms Lab
python_ml_projects.summary: Catalogue of 11 end-to-end ML projects: regression (linear, polynomial), classification (logistic, KNN, decision trees, random forests, SVM), clustering (K-means), dimensionality reduction (PCA), NLP (Naive Bayes), deep learning (Keras), recommender systems. Per-project Jupyter notebooks documenting problem, methods, decisions, and results.

python_oop_minisystems.title: Python OOP Mini-Systems
python_oop_minisystems.summary: Six compact Python systems demonstrating OOP design: Tic-Tac-Toe (procedural), Blackjack (composition), Credit Card Validator (Luhn algorithm), Bank Account Manager (inheritance), Product Inventory (CRUD), Library Lending (item subclasses). Sequenced from procedural to inheritance.

digital_twin.title: Digital Twin — This Conversational Agent
digital_twin.summary: The very system you are interacting with. Multi-LLM conversational agent representing Alejandro professionally. Classify-then-route orchestration, Frame/Substance prompt composition, branch-aware tool use with bounded loop, multi-LLM guardrail with structured retry, deterministic short-circuits, full per-turn observability logs, ADR-driven discipline. Use this key for questions about the chatbot itself.

gcb_2025_physiological_stress.title: GCB 2025 — Climate-Induced Physiological Stress (Ringtail Possums)
gcb_2025_physiological_stress.summary: First-author Global Change Biology 2025 paper. Four-component unified Bayesian framework (microclimate + physiological energetics + biogeochemical pathway + open population model) integrated in R+JAGS, bridging bottom-up biophysical and top-down statistical modelling for causal attribution of population decline to climate stress.

dd_2022_ringtail_possums_climate.title: DD 2022 — Ringtail Possums Climate Forecast
dd_2022_ringtail_possums_climate.summary: First-author Diversity and Distributions 2022 paper. Bayesian hierarchical open population model on 30 years of monitoring (1992–2021), forecasting collapse probability by 2050 under projected heatwave/warming scenarios. Detection-probability correction; PVA over multiple viability thresholds. Used to support EPBC Act re-listing.

dd_2022_vertebrate_communities.title: DD 2022 — Vertebrate Communities under Elevational Shifts
dd_2022_vertebrate_communities.summary: First-author Diversity and Distributions 2022 paper. Spatially explicit dispersal simulation of 7,613 community assemblages (47 vertebrate species) using thermal resistance as movement friction. Quantifies the 'escalator to extinction' spatial signature via beta-diversity dissimilarity from heterogeneous per-species dispersal success.

ncc_mountains.title: Mountains Magnify Mechanisms in Climate Change Biology (Nature Climate Change 2026)
ncc_mountains.summary: First-author Nature Climate Change Perspective. Argues that compressed climatic gradients of mountain elevations function as natural experimental contrasts, making mountains uniquely powerful systems for detecting causal mechanisms of climate-driven biological change. Theoretical-framework paper, not new empirical data.

delafuente_2021_ecography.title: Predicting Species Abundance by Implementing the Ecological Niche Theory (Ecography 2021)
delafuente_2021_ecography.summary: First-author Ecography 2021 paper. Tests whether SDM-derived habitat suitability predicts observed abundance for 50 endemic species across 29 years of monitoring. 9-algorithm SDM ensemble, multiple link functions, spatial blocked cross-validation. Mean 55% deviance explained — first systematic large-scale test for tropical endemics.

williams_delafuente_2021_plosone.title: Long-Term Changes in Populations of Rainforest Birds in the Australia Wet Tropics Bioregion (PLOS One 2021)
williams_delafuente_2021_plosone.summary: Co-authored PLoS ONE 2021 paper. 17 years of bird monitoring across 114 sites (47 species, sea level to 1500 m) documenting >40% lower-edge abundance loss for upland species, up to 190% upward expansion of lowland species, ~50% upland-endemic decline. Empirical evidence the Wet Tropics World Heritage Area's Outstanding Universal Value is degrading.

bosque_2017_chusquea.title: Biomass, Seed Production and Phenology of Chusquea montana — Puyehue National Park, Chile (Bosque 2017)
bosque_2017_chusquea.summary: First-author Bosque 2017 field study quantifying the spring-2015 mass-flowering event of Chusquea montana bamboo. 8 plots × 25 m² for biomass, 20 seed collection boxes for productivity/viability, repeated phenological observations. Results: 33.50 Mg ha⁻¹ fresh biomass, 146.86 × 10⁶ seeds ha⁻¹ peak production, 87.5% viability — first species-level phenological description for C. montana.

gcb_2023_rainforest_birds_climate.title: The Climatic Drivers of Long-Term Population Changes in Rainforest Montane Birds (Global Change Biology 2023)
gcb_2023_rainforest_birds_climate.summary: First-author GCB 2023 paper decomposing climate-change effects on 47 rainforest bird species into multiple distinct stressors: long-term warming, rainfall change, extreme heatwaves, droughts, and cyclone-induced canopy damage (NDVI from Landsat 5/8). Bayesian N-mixture model in R + JAGS over 2000–2016 across 124 survey locations / 24 sites. Key finding: 72% of species show significant temperature response with strong elevational asymmetry (lowlands benefit, uplands decline). Sister paper to Williams & de la Fuente 2021 — that one shows the redistribution pattern, this one identifies the climatic mechanisms.

oecologia_2024_herbivory.title: Relationships Between Abiotic Factors, Foliage Chemistry and Herbivory in a Tropical Montane Ecosystem (Oecologia 2024)
oecologia_2024_herbivory.summary: First-author Oecologia 2024 paper tracing the causal chain climate + geology → soil nutrients → foliar chemistry → insect herbivory across the Australian Wet Tropics. 25 sites covering 94% of bioregional climate variability across basalt/rhyolite/granite parent materials, 3 widespread tree species. Hierarchical Bayesian pathway model in R + JAGS recovers direct + indirect effects in one joint posterior. Key finding: once geological origin is controlled, individual soil nutrients are equivocal predictors of foliar composition; species-specific responses dominate.

siri_2025_forest_gap_birds.title: The Effect of Forest Gap Dynamics on Tropical Rainforest Birds (Ecologica Montenegrina 2025)
siri_2025_forest_gap_birds.summary: Co-authored Ecologica Montenegrina 2025 paper (Siri et al.; de la Fuente led the analytical framework). 5-year mist-netting study (2015–2019) in a Thai lower-montane 16-ha plot, 1,148 captures of 81 species, paired forest-gap vs closed-canopy sites. GLMMs (Poisson, lme4) for species-level abundance, PCA for community structure, ANOVA for gap-size effects. Key findings: total abundance unchanged but assemblage composition shifts; Hill Blue Flycatcher gap-specialist (β=0.35, p=0.01); moderate gaps (130–1,020 m²) reduce abundance for sensitive species without being catastrophic.
